About This Dashboard
What It Does
Short-term momentum ranker scoring 700+ tickers on returns (1d to 1y), SMA gate (above 30/50/100/200 day), ratio consistency across timeframes, and resilience on bad SPY days. Higher composite scores indicate stronger, more consistent short-term momentum.
Today's Reading
# Market Analysis: momentum-ranker (Today's Data) CLMT ranks #1 with an exceptional composite score of 93.5, driven by extraordinary consistency across all timeframes—168% annual returns with a perfect 4-of-5 ratio passes and all SMAs above price—but shows critical warning signs of overextension with a momentum signal of 2.29 and near-perfect resilience on bad SPY days (0.9864 score), suggesting the move may have exhausted near-term catalysts. SRZN ranks #2 with similar long-term strength (163.94% 1-year return) yet exhibits concerning ratio inconsistency (only 3-of-5 passes) and poor bad-SPY resilience (0.2306 score), indicating vulnerability to market reversals and significantly weaker downside protection than CLMT despite comparable annual gains. The data snapshot appears truncated at SRZN's correlation details, but the contrast between these two leaders highlights a critical distinction: CLMT's overextension comes with defensive qualities, while SRZN's momentum lacks market-regime confirmation, making SRZN the riskier proposition despite both passing all price-level gates. Both tickers have dramatically outpaced major indices (SPY, QQQ) on a 1-year basis, but the divergence in volatility
